parameters.name: funcrgba
parameters.label: Function RGBA
parameters.description: Applies the selected function to the R, G, B, and A channels.

parameters.name: funcrgb
parameters.label: Function RGB
parameters.description: Applies the selected function to the R, G, and B channels.

parameters.name: funcr
parameters.label: Function R
parameters.description: Applies the selected function to the R (red) channel.

parameters.name: funcg
parameters.label: Function G
parameters.description: Applies the selected function to the G (green) channel.

parameters.name: funcb
parameters.label: Function B
parameters.description: Applies the selected function to the B (blue) channel.

parameters.name: funca
parameters.label: Function A
parameters.description: Applies the selected function to the A (alpha) channel.

parameters.name: baseval
parameters.label: Base Value
parameters.description: Supplies the base value for functions like 'Log Base N' and 'Base ^ Input'

parameters.name: expval
parameters.label: Exponent Value
parameters.description: Supplies the exponent value for the function 'Input ^ Base'.

parameters.name: constval
parameters.label: Constant Value
parameters.description: Allows setting the output to a specific constant value using the 'Constant' function.

parameters.name: angunit
parameters.label: Angle Units
parameters.description: Determines whether the input values are measured in degrees, radians, etc for functions that require an angle input e.g. sine, cosine, etc.

parameters.name: replace
parameters.label: Replace Errors
parameters.description: When enabled, output values that would otherwise be invalid will be replaced with the value of the 'Error Value' parameter. For example, when using the log function, the output will be replaced whenev

parameters.name: errval
parameters.label: Error Value
parameters.description: The output value to use when an input error is detected e.g. log(-1).

parameters.name: rerange2
parameters.label: Rerange2
parameters.description: Applies a scale and shift to the input values before the function is calculated i.e. input = (input * rerange2) + rerange1. Note: This feature only affects integer texture formats and is not used on f
